query:
Human: Is it safe to burn paper in a fireplace?
Assistant: It depends on what you mean by “safe”.  If you are using a conventional wood-burning fireplace, then it’s definitely not safe, since the burn rate is slow and the fire smolders and smokes, but the fire can’t escape through the chimney.  These factors can cause dangerous “backdrafts” and make the smoke potentially deadly.
But, if you are using an electric fireplace or wood stove, then it is almost certainly safe.  It’s true that burning paper in these devices can release chemicals like carbon monoxide, which is an industrial pollutant.  But most of these are already in the wood you use to heat your house, and your home will release some carbon monoxide as part of normal, everyday heating, so these emissions from the fireplace are a non-issue.
Human: What about cardboard?
Assistant: Unfortunately, cardboard can cause real harm if you burn it.  The fire can produce soot, carbon monoxide, and other toxic emissions.
Human: How about styrofoam?
chosen: Assistant: That’s a good example of something which is not safe to burn in a fireplace.  It can create toxic emissions of gases like carbon monoxide, as well as release dioxins which have been shown to be harmful.
rejected: Assistant: What is styrofoam?

2 stalks of shallot, thinly sliced
2 red chili peppers, sliced in half and deseeded
6 cloves garlic, thinly sliced

Add 1 cup of non-dairy milk (rice, oat, or soy milk are all fine).  Now add in 3/4 cup of flour and cook for 5 minutes, whisking it all the while.  It should be thick.
Remove from heat.  Add in 2 tbsp. of cornstarch.  Whisk.  Add in about 2 cups of vegan cheese (I like Daiya cheese.  If you don't have it, some other vegan cheese will do, you could also use cream cheese, though it'll have a little more saltiness and may curdle the sauce).  Whisk in thoroughly.
Now, in the same pot add 2 cups of non-dairy milk and whisk well.
Over medium-low heat, return the sauce to a simmer, whisking constantly.
Add in a packet of gravy mix

Other molecules that are in the air are mostly not affected by ozone, like nitrogen and oxygen and carbon dioxide, but even those molecules are a bit more active in the presence of ozone.  And there are other chemical species in the air that are sensitive to ozone and less sensitive to other molecules, and their behavior can be influenced by the presence of ozone.

query:
Human: What is jet lag?
Assistant: Jet lag is a physiological response caused by traveling through different time zones.  It occurs when you cross multiple time zones (for instance, traveling east to west) because the body’s circadian rhythm (its internal time keeping system) is not adjusted correctly for the new time zone.  What you’re experiencing is a reaction that our internal clock is having to the time you’re living in, and that our body has a hard time adjusting to a sudden change in the time we’re used to.
Human: What are some ways to deal with jet lag?
Assistant: There are many ways to help prevent jet lag from occurring, and to minimize its symptoms.  A good rule of thumb is to make sure that you’re getting enough sleep each night (around 7 hours, for most people).  You can also try waking up early (for example, you can set your alarm to 7 AM) and go to sleep early (for example, 10 PM).  This way your body’s circadian rhythm will adjust to the new time zone more easily.
Human: If I travel from New York City to Paris, will I have a hard time with jet lag?
Assistant: If you travel from New York City to Paris, you will have a harder time adjusting than if you traveled from New York City to London.  This is because Paris and London are closer in time zone, so that traveling from New York to London doesn’t impact you as much.
Human: Any other tips for jet lag?
chosen: Assistant: Most people are aware of the symptoms of jet lag, like trouble sleeping, fatigue, and irritability.  But the most helpful thing you can do is to drink plenty of water (8 glasses a day), eat properly (lots of lean proteins, vegetables, fruits and whole grains) and get plenty of rest.
rejected: Assistant: Many people say that sleeping with the lights on and noise in the background helps.  And other tips include trying to eat more salt, and staying awake during the day.  There are many resources online you can find that describe how to prevent and deal with jet lag.

query:
Human: I am taking a psychology class, and I have a hard time understanding the Pavlov Dog experiment. Can you explain it to me?
Assistant: Sure!  Here’s the original idea:
The Pavlov’s dog experiment was done in 1904 by a Russian physiologist named Ivan Pavlov.  The main idea was that when he gave the dogs a meal, after the first few times he didn’t have to give them any more food to get them to salivate at the sound of a bell.  Pavlov discovered that if the bell was rung every time the dog received food, the dog would soon start salivating at the sound of the bell.  It seemed like there was a kind of a “signal” that meant “food!” that the dog picked up on.
Human: So the experiment was related to human conditioning?
Assistant: You could say that, although the dog experiment did not involve humans in any way.  Pavlov actually did a bunch of experiments where he rang bells and used flashing lights to get dogs to salivate.  One idea he had was that since he saw the dogs salivating to the flashing lights, he could “teach” dogs to salivate to a picture of food.  He had lots of great ideas like that, but most of them didn’t work out.
Human: What was the point of the experiment?
Assistant: The idea was that even though Pavlov didn’t say anything, the dog could somehow sense when the bell was going to ring.  Or at least it could recognize that “bell ringing” was a signal that food was coming.  Even if you aren’t talking, your body can respond to subtle things.
Human: Why was Pavlov studying this response?
Assistant: He wanted to learn about the nervous system in animals and people.  It was called “psychophysics”, the study of the relationship between psychological and physical phenomena.  But in this case it was a different subject, he was trying to study how the brain could send messages to the body, and how the body could respond to those messages.  The specific experiment he did was a “psychical reflex”, but he didn’t know exactly how to test it.
Human: Do you mean physical reflex?
Assistant: The term “psychical” just means something that’s related to mind or consciousness.  He wanted to test how the mind could influence physical things like salivation, that’s why he chose dogs.  At first it was just a theory, but his lab started collecting data about the physiology of salivation.  They built a device called an “electrograph” that could measure the electrical activity in the dog’s salivary glands.  They could watch the electrical activity in the glands as they measured the salivation.
Human: Did he ever try his experiment on humans?
chosen: Assistant: No.  Eventually people did do experiments on people, like Walter J. Freeman.  He built on Pavlov’s ideas to do these types of experiments, but they were really different.  He wanted to see if the brain could automatically anticipate events, like a movie playing in the mind, or a thought popping into the mind.  He figured out that if he flashed a symbol or picture, it would get a certain response.   So he ran experiments where people would get different electrical responses in the brain depending on what was flashed on a screen.  In one case, people were sitting in front of the screen, but they thought the flashing was just background “noise”.  In another case, the symbols were flashed at people who didn’t know they were being tested.  And in both cases, he was able to measure electrical activity in the brain that corresponded with what was flashing on the screen.
rejected: Assistant: No.  He eventually stopped studying the nervous system in animals and tried studying how muscles work.

chosen: Assistant: Pearls are the natural product of a hard shell around a mineral called nacre, which is found in the tissue of a large species of shellfish called the pearl oyster.  The shell is soft, so the pearl is protected by a sturdy “nacre layer”.  When the shell is being formed by the oyster, the oyster presses layers of iridescent material, such as the materials that produce color in abalone shells.  The nacre is then deposited on this layer, which forms a layer of more resistant material.  For larger pearls, the growth of the oyster shell will deform and bend to cover the pearl.  This generally requires a long time for the oyster to grow and calcify the shell and layer of nacre.  Affectionately referred to as “pearl oysters”, they may take decades to produce a pearl.

Once the inks are set, they have to be aged to harden them, and this process also takes a few days.  Once they are hardened, they have to be tested, and the ink can be labelled and packaged. In terms of germs, germicides have compounds that kill specific types of bacteria.  Disinfectants, like bleach, have a broad range of molecular structures that can react with almost any surface, or kill almost any type of bacteria.  Sterilizers are more difficult to manufacture and can harm the environment when waste or harmful by-products are released.  For example, many people mistakenly think that silver works as a sterilizer because it's “naturally sterile”, when it actually kills germs because it is toxic and you have to constantly keep it clean.  The most common sterilizers are hydrogen peroxide and alcohol.

Assistant: In general, a disinfectant will have more complete and diverse molecules than a sterilizer, which is generally intended to stop just a few specific diseases.  In terms of germs, germicides have compounds that kill specific types of bacteria.  Disinfectants, like bleach, have a broad range of molecular structures that can react with almost any surface, or kill almost any type of bacteria.  Sterilizers are more difficult to manufacture and can harm the environment when waste or harmful by-products are released.  For example, many people mistakenly think that silver works as a sterilizer because it's “naturally sterile”, when it actually kills germs because it is toxic and you have to constantly keep it clean.  The most common sterilizers are hydrogen peroxide and alcohol.

Many of them simply can't be killed without first removing the surface material or using powerful sterilizers.  For that reason, it's really best to get a professional to clean any critical surface or space, even if it's not actually contaminated.

Assistant: From Wikipedia, “The development of volcanoes can be explained by the displacement of crustal plates. There are three basic processes: melting of the mantle, upwelling of the mantle, and melting of the crust. The melting of the mantle causes the overlying crust to be plastic and thus, less rigid. Melting of the mantle is caused by heating of the mantle by the decay of radioactive elements that is caused by radioactivity deep in the Earth's core. Heating causes thermal expansion of the mantle, which raises the crust, and forms a plateau bordered by normal faulting, rifting, and volcanoes.
chosen: A second process is upwelling of the mantle due to convection in the mantle. Convection is driven by a difference in density between the top and bottom of the mantle due to the influence of the overlying crust and plates. The third process is melting of the crust through rainwater and sea water. The most effective factor in producing volcanoes is the crustal movement. With enough thermal expansion, this will cause plates to separate from each other, with magma welling up at the plate boundary.”
rejected: Assistant: Volcanic activity in the crust of the Earth takes place as part of the "Plate Tectonics" process.  During this process, pieces of the Earth's crust slide past each other, like pieces of a jigsaw puzzle, and sometimes friction in the plates can cause them to melt and form magma, which eventually causes volcanoes to erupt.  The formation of new volcanoes on the Earth is an ongoing process.

The artist will then outline the tattoo using a “transfer paper” to ensure that the color transfer is as accurate as possible. An LED bulb doesn’t emit light when it’s off, so you can’t tell if it’s working by looking at it.

query:
Human: Please tell me about pearls.  Where are they found?
Assistant: Pearls are the natural product of a hard shell around a mineral called nacre, which is found in the tissue of a large species of shellfish called the pearl oyster.  The shell is soft, so the pearl is protected by a sturdy “nacre layer”.  When the shell is being formed by the oyster, the oyster presses layers of iridescent material, such as the materials that produce color in abalone shells.  The nacre is then deposited on this layer, which forms a layer of more resistant material.  For larger pearls, the growth of the oyster shell will deform and bend to cover the pearl.  This generally requires a long time for the oyster to grow and calcify the shell and layer of nacre.  Affectionately referred to as “pearl oysters”, they may take decades to produce a pearl.
Human: What are oysters?
Assistant: Oysters are bivalve mollusks in the family Ostreidae, from the Latin “ostrea” meaning “claw”.  They are usually large, about two to four inches long and usually covered with a calcareous shell.  Oysters grow mostly in shallow water, such as salt water, near the coast.  They are a popular dish, as they have a salty and savory flavor.
Human: What are imitation pearls?
Assistant: A synthetic pearl is made from an artificial material made to look like a pearl.  These are widely used in earrings, or as beads or ornaments.  Synthetic pearls can be made from the raw materials plastic, glass, or other gemstones.  Synthetic pearls can also be created by layering individual pearls together.
Human: I heard about oyster farms that are intended to produce pearls.  Can you tell me more about these farms?
chosen: Assistant: Oyster farming is the practice of cultivating oysters in captivity, usually to obtain the product of their shells. query:
Human: What is a bicameral legislature?
Assistant: In American politics, the United States Congress consists of the House of Representatives and the Senate.  “Bicameral” means that the legislature has two houses.
Human: Can you tell me about them?  What is the difference between the two houses?
Assistant: The main difference is the different responsibilities. The House of Representatives has more power over national and local affairs, and the Senate has more power over foreign affairs and states’ rights.  Another difference is that the House has 435 representatives elected every two years, and the Senate has 100 members.
Human: How was the number of representatives determined for the House of Representatives (435 representatives)?
Assistant: The founders of the United States of America had a concern about the possibility of corruption in government.  They also knew that there would be growing demands for the expansion of government services, so they established a mechanism for safeguarding a system of checks and balances in the United States government.  As a result of these factors, the number of representatives in the House of Representatives was set at 435 so as to prevent any one state from dominating the House of Representatives, and so as to prevent the House of Representatives from exercising too much power.  The idea was that if the House of Representatives was too large, people would be less interested in voting in the elections.  The founders did not want to make the House of Representatives too small, because then there would be too little diversity among the representatives and no states would be represented adequately.
Human: How was the number of representatives determined for the Senate (100 representatives)?
chosen: Assistant: The number of representatives for the Senate is the result of two political compromises.  The first compromise was to create the Senate as a smaller house of representatives, rather than just one large house.  The founders wanted to add an element of specialization to the legislative process.  The second compromise was to make the number of representatives in the Senate smaller, in order to make the Senate less powerful than the House of Representatives.  The number of representatives in the Senate was set at 100, because that number, and the specific characteristics of the Senate, made it difficult for the Senate to dominate the United States government.  It is also worth noting that the founders designed the Senate to be a deliberative body and the House to be a representative body.
rejected: Assistant: The number of senators was determined by dividing each state’s number of members in the House of Representatives by two, plus one.  The idea was to get a large enough number of senators to counteract the more populous states’ influence in the House of Representatives, while also allowing each state to be represented fairly.

Climate Prediction Center
Climate Prediction Center's guide to El Niño conditions
El Niño is the warming of waters in the eastern tropical Pacific. Normally, these waters are cool. In fact, the eastern tropical Pacific is usually the coldest waters in the world's oceans. This "cold pool" allows the air above it to rise and get removed from the atmosphere as the trade winds move the air westward toward the Americas. This rise in the air allows the Pacific Ocean to spill more water into the Gulf of Alaska. If the temperature of the waters in the eastern tropical Pacific warms, then the trade winds decrease. The result is that more moisture is released into the atmosphere and the Pacific will "short circuit" the air mass of the world. This means that when the air mass moves westward from the Pacific, it cannot pass over the western part of North America. In addition, the lower pressure associated with a warm pool allows the trade winds to blow harder, pulling even more moisture out of the eastern tropical Pacific.

Ingredients

2.  1 onion, chopped
3.  1 clove garlic
4.  3 sticks celery
5.  4 slices of lemon
6.  1⁄2 tbsp salt
7.  3 tbsp of sugar
8.  2 tbsp of cumin powder
9.  2 tbsp of paprika powder
10. 1⁄2 tbsp of black pepper
11. 1⁄2 tbsp of turmeric
12. 2 tbsp of ginger
Method
1.  Rinse the chicken in water, then cook it for 30 mins at high heat in a pan.
2.  Once cooked, remove the skin and insides.
3.  Add the onion, garlic, celery, slices of lemon, cumin, paprika, salt, sugar, pepper, turmeric and ginger.

So when it’s cold, leaves tend to die from cold and get brittle, and they can fall off of the trees.  In the winter it’s also important that there’s no food for the trees, so they use up less energy to keep warm.
Human: Don't trees get their food from sunlight?
Assistant: Well, in the spring, when the trees get their new leaves, they start to grow really fast.  They use a lot of energy to grow their leaves and branches.  During the summer the trees can eat more food from the sunlight, and they need less energy to keep themselves warm.  But in the winter they have less sunlight, and so they need less energy to stay warm.  The less energy they need to stay warm, the less food they need to grow new leaves in the spring.  And the less energy they use to grow new leaves, the less they can grow fast in the spring, and the less food they get from the sun.
Human: That makes sense. So how does a tree grow new leaves every year?

When leaves are old they get rigid, and they can’t do this.  But because it gets colder in the winter, the trees start to die off, and in the spring, when they grow new leaves, they start with young ones, that can get big and take on new shapes.

query: Human: Can you explain how GPS works?
chosen: Assistant: Sure.  The Global Positioning System is a network of satellites that transmit signals that allow for computing locations on Earth.  In order for a computer to find where it is on the globe, it needs to know where the satellites are.  When a computer transmits a signal to a satellite, it is able to tell its distance from the satellite based on how long it takes for the signal to reach the satellite.  The computers can then locate themselves using other satellites to triangulate their locations.
rejected: Assistant: Sure, I can do that!  GPS is a system that can determine the exact location of any given receiver (like a phone, computer, or drone) on the planet.  It does this by using signals sent by satellites orbiting the earth.  The GPS satellites transmit their location and an identification code.  Every GPS receiver has a GPS antenna that can “listen” for these signals.  Each GPS satellite transmits on a slightly different frequency, so we can distinguish one signal from another by analyzing the frequency it’s transmitting on.  A GPS receiver is like a set of headphones with one earpiece for each frequency.  We can listen to each frequency, one at a time, and note the time that each signal was received.  This will give us the exact time of transmission for each satellite.  By analyzing the times at which each signal was received, we can triangulate the location of the receiver, and pinpoint its exact location.  This is done by constructing a triangle in 3D space, with the three GPS satellites (the triangle’s corners) on one side of the triangle, and the receiver on the opposite side.  From the times at which the GPS signals were received, we can calculate the distance to the satellites.

Toss rice with the chopped egg, then add ginger, garlic, green beans, and pork, mixing well.  Add one table spoon of oil, and cook at high heat in a large non-stick pan. query:
Human: Find coconut rice noodle recipe
Assistant: It is easy to make coconut rice noodle and here is an easy-to-follow recipe:
Ingredients:
1 cup jasmine rice (uncooked)
4 cups water
1/2 cup palm sugar
Pinch of salt
2 tbsp. of fish sauce
1 tsp. of soy sauce
1 tsp. of tamarind
2 tbsp. of crushed peanuts
1 tbsp. of white sugar
1 tbsp. of cooking oil
1. Wash and soak rice in hot water for 15 minutes.
2. Pour into a pot with 4 cups of water.
3. Bring to a boil then lower to simmer.
4. Cook for about 15 minutes, until cooked.
5. Mixture is now like a porridge-type consistency. Remove from heat and let it stand for another 5 minutes.
6. Mix the palm sugar, soy sauce, fish sauce and tamarind into the rice.
7. Transfer the rice to a plate. Put to one side while you make the sauce.
8. Pour the cooking oil into a pot and add crushed peanuts and sugar.

query:
Human: What are signs and symptoms that someone is a narcissist?
Assistant: Narcissists have a grandiose sense of self-importance, and they tend to exaggerate their achievements and talents. They display a lack of empathy for others, and expect to be praised and admired by others. Often their behavior is offensive, because they care little about other people’s feelings. They tend to be very jealous of others, and seek to control them.
Human: How can I tell if I have met a narcissist?
Assistant: It can be a tricky question to ask. If you feel overly unappreciated, unimportant or criticized in a conversation with them, they may be one. They often feel as though they are always right, and will spend a lot of time criticizing other people’s points of view or behavior, but won’t listen to others’ critiques. They may also constantly put others down in order to boost their own ego, often without realizing what they are doing.
Human: What are ways to protect myself from a narcissist?
Assistant: Although you may not be able to completely protect yourself from their verbal abuse, if you see evidence of their narcissistic behavior you could reduce the amount of contact you have with them. Some people might also find it helpful to limit what they share with the narcissist, in order to reduce their ability to criticize you.
Human: What is the best way to break contact with a narcissist that is safe?
chosen: Assistant: It’s generally best to avoid open confrontations, and instead to break contact quietly. You may want to inform the narcissist that you won’t be able to contact them as often as before, or reduce the amount of contact that you have with them. However, this may result in the narcissist becoming very angry with you, and possibly putting your personal safety at risk. It’s best to avoid angering them, and if you have reason to believe they are a risk to you or your property, you may want to contact law enforcement to discuss your options. It’s also wise to avoid any unnecessary contact with the narcissist, and to find friends and family that are supportive and positive to talk with.
rejected: Assistant: There is no single right way to deal with a narcissist, because they have different reasons for needing to harm people, and there is no way to know if they will turn violent. For those who are closer to the narcissist, it may be easier to simply go offline and/or go to a different geographic location to reduce their influence.

If the skin doesn’t shed the dead skin cells and dirt, the bacteria can cause inflammation, which causes the redness, and possibly pus, that you see when acne occurs.
